title: Melted Spicy Venezuelan Red Potatoes Casserole
description: A spicy and tender Venezuelan dish featuring red potatoes, beurre-monte, and kosher-friendly ingredients, perfect for midnight cravings.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the sliced red potatoes and cold water to cover, and let it sit for 30 minutes to remove excess starch.
Drain the potatoes and pat them dry with paper towels to remove excess moisture.
In a large saucepan, melt the unsalted butter over medium heat, then add the beurre-monte, hot sauce, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper, and stir until smooth.
In a greased 9x13 inch baking dish, create a layer of potatoes, then pour some of the butter mixture over the potatoes, and sprinkle with shredded cheese and chopped cilantro.
Repeat the layering process until all the ingredients are used, ending with a layer of cheese on top.
Cover the dish with aluminum foil and bake for 45 minutes.
Remove the foil and continue baking for an additional 20 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the potatoes are tender.
Remove the dish from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
